section: How it works · Order → Inventory-out → Ledger
lede: The mirror image of procure-to-pay. A sales order is a customer's intent to buy; placing it only RESERVES stock. Fulfilment is the resolution — it moves inventory OUT, consumes cost layers into COGS, and books revenue, output VAT, and a receivable in one stroke. Follow an order from placed to delivered and watch six ledger effects fall out of a single fulfil action.

steps.title: Orders is the sell-side lifecycle
steps.narration: Every customer order for the active branch, with its payment and fulfilment status. Placing an order does NOT move stock or post revenue — it records intent and RESERVES the units. Reservation is a soft hold: available on-hand drops, but nothing has physically left and nothing has hit the ledger. Cart abandonment releases the hold (and re-syncs the storefront projection).

steps.title: Open the order — Payment, Delivery, Shipping
steps.narration: The detail sheet splits the order into its lifecycle: Payment (was it collected — prepaid, or COD to reconcile later), Delivery/Shipping (how it leaves), and the line items + totals. Each transition here is a trigger that resolves into stock and the ledger — the buttons are the least of it.

steps.title: Ship / fulfil the order
steps.narration: Ship Order is the moment intent becomes a transaction. It is one click, but it fires the two resolutions below — inventory and ledger — atomically, so stock and books can never disagree about a sale.

steps.title: Fulfilment resolves INVENTORY out
steps.tag: stock loc → customer loc; cost layers → COGS
steps.narration: Flow runs a move from the STOCK location to the CUSTOMER (virtual) location: the quant decrements, the earlier reservation is CONSUMED, and the specific COST LAYERS the units draw from (FIFO/AVCO) are what set the cost of the sale. This is why receiving opened cost layers — selling closes them. Available and on-hand both drop; the storefront projection re-syncs.

steps.title: Fulfilment resolves the LEDGER
steps.tag: Dr COGS 5111 / Cr Inventory · Dr A/R 1141 / Cr Revenue 4111 + Output VAT 2132
steps.narration: Two postings land here. At COST: Dr COGS (5111) / Cr Inventory (1164) — the value leaves the balance sheet. At PRICE: Dr Accounts Receivable (1141) — or Cash for a paid/POS sale — / Cr Sales Revenue (4111) + Cr Output VAT (2132). Inventory leaves at cost, revenue books at price; the gap is your gross margin, computed from the exact cost layers consumed above.

steps.title: Revenue flows to VAT + receivables
steps.narration: The output VAT credited to 2132 is the same figure that rolls into the Mushak 9.1 monthly VAT return, and a Mushak 6.3 tax invoice is auto-generated on fulfilment. The A/R debit sits on A/R Aging with its due date until the customer pays (Dr Cash / Cr A/R). One fulfil action therefore resolves into six connected effects — stock out, reservation consumed, COGS, revenue, output VAT, and a receivable — every one traceable to this order.
